title: Spergularia denticulata
taxonomy: Spergularia denticulata belongs to the family Caryophyllaceae. It is a species of flowering plant in the genus Spergularia. Spergularia denticulata is a prostrate, annual herb with small white flowers. This species is native to Europe and Western Asia, but has been naturalized in other parts of the world. It is also known as saltwort or sea sand-spurrey, due to its ability to thrive in coastal areas.
uses: Spergularia denticulata, also known as coastal sandspurry, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory properties and to treat various health issues. The plant's extracts have been studied for their antioxidant and antimicrobial effects, which may contribute to its medicinal uses.
